seo.title: Theater Detail API - JKT48 Show Lineups, Setlists & Seitansai
seo.description: Access comprehensive data for individual JKT48 theater shows — member lineups, setlist details, seitansai celebrations, and ticket information. Perfect for show detail pages and performance tracking.

hero.heading: What's in tonight's show?
hero.subheading: Comprehensive data for any individual JKT48 theater show — full member lineup, setlist details, seitansai celebrations, and ticket information. Everything you need to build rich show detail pages.

definition.title: What is the Theater Detail API?
definition.text:
The Theater Detail API provides comprehensive information about a single JKT48 theater show, identified by its `show_id` from the Theater Schedule API.

Where the Theater API gives you a list of shows with summary data, Theater Detail goes deeper — giving you everything there is to know about one specific performance.

The Theater Detail response includes:

- **Full member lineup** — the complete list of all members performing in the show, not just senbatsu starters
- **Setlist details** — the show title, setlist name, and show description
- **Seitansai (birthday celebration)** — if the show features a member's birthday celebration, the honored member and celebration details are included
- **Show type** — regular show, birthday show (seitansai), special show, request hour, or unit show
- **Date & time** — exact show time, door opening time, and show start time
- **Ticket information** — ticket status, lottery details, and ticket purchase links where available
- **Venue details** — theater name, floor, and location
- **MC and special segments** — additional show segments or special announcements where available

This endpoint is the foundation for building comprehensive show detail pages, seitansai trackers, and any feature that needs complete per-show data rather than the summary provided by the Theater list endpoint.

capabilities_section.title: What you can do with Theater Detail
capabilities_section.intro: Build rich show detail pages, seitansai trackers, and member lineup features with per-show granular data.

capabilities.title: Full member lineup
capabilities.description: Access the complete list of all members performing in the show — beyond the senbatsu starters. See every member on stage for that specific performance.

capabilities.title: Seitansai (birthday celebration) data
capabilities.description: When a show features a member's seitansai, the Theater Detail response includes the honored member, celebration type, and any special birthday show details.

capabilities.title: Setlist & show description
capabilities.description: Get the full setlist name, show title, and description — the complete identity of the performance beyond just the team and date.

capabilities.title: Ticket status & purchase links
capabilities.description: Access current ticket availability, lottery status, and direct purchase links where officially available — ready to display to fans visiting a show detail page.

capabilities.title: Show timing details
capabilities.description: Access door opening time and show start time alongside the date — all the information fans need to plan their attendance.

capabilities.title: Linked via show_id
capabilities.description: Uses the show_id field from the Theater API response as the lookup key — making it easy to enrich any theater schedule entry with full show detail on demand.

how_it_works.title: How the Theater Detail endpoint works
how_it_works.intro: Two steps: get the show_id from the Theater API, then fetch the full detail.

steps.title: Get a show_id from the Theater API
steps.description: Call GET /v1/theater to get the upcoming show schedule. Each show entry includes a show_id that uniquely identifies that performance.

steps.title: Call the Theater Detail endpoint
steps.description: Make a GET request to /v1/theater/:show_id with your API key and the show_id from step one. The response returns comprehensive detail for that specific show.

steps.title: Display the full show page
steps.description: Use the member lineup, setlist info, seitansai data, and ticket details to build a complete show detail page — everything fans need to know about a specific performance.

use_cases.title: Who uses Theater Detail
use_cases.intro: Fan developers building show detail pages and theater-focused tracking features.

items.title: Show detail pages
items.description: Build comprehensive show detail pages that display the full member lineup, setlist name, seitansai info, show timing, and ticket status — giving fans everything they need to decide whether to attend or follow along.

items.title: Seitansai trackers
items.description: Build dedicated seitansai tracking features that highlight upcoming birthday shows, the honored member, and celebration details. Let fans plan ahead for their oshi's seitansai.

items.title: Member appearance detail
items.description: Use Theater Detail to show fans not just which shows a member appeared in, but the full context — the complete lineup, the setlist, and whether it was a special show type.

items.title: Ticket availability pages
items.description: Build show pages with live ticket status and direct purchase links — combining Theater Detail with periodic polling or webhooks to keep availability status current.

related_features.title: Theater Schedule
related_features.description: Start here to get show_id values from the upcoming schedule. Theater Detail enriches any show entry from this endpoint.

related_features.title: Members
related_features.description: Theater Detail member lineup entries reference member records. Use the Members API to enrich each lineup entry with photos, generation info, and social links.

related_features.title: Member Detail
related_features.description: Member Detail includes upcoming theater appearances. Combine with Theater Detail for the most complete show and member data.

related_features.title: Events
related_features.description: Special theater events like seitansai and request hour shows also appear in the Events API for a different view of the same data.

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions
faqs.intro: Common questions about the Theater Detail API.

items.question: Where do I get the show_id from?
items.answer: The show_id is included in every show entry returned by the Theater API (/v1/theater). Call the Theater schedule endpoint first to get a list of upcoming shows, then use any show_id from that response to fetch full detail from this endpoint.

items.question: What is seitansai and how is it represented in the API?
items.answer: Seitansai is a birthday celebration show where the theater show is dedicated to a member celebrating their birthday. In the Theater Detail response, seitansai shows include a seitansai field with the honored member's details and celebration info. Non-seitansai shows return null for this field.

items.question: Does Theater Detail include the full member lineup or only senbatsu?
items.answer: Theater Detail includes the complete performing member list for the show — all members on stage, not just the senbatsu starters. The Theater list endpoint returns only the senbatsu summary; Theater Detail gives you the full lineup.

items.question: How current is the ticket status data?
items.answer: Ticket status is updated as changes are announced officially by JKT48. The status reflects the last known state — available, sold out, lottery, or not yet on sale. For time-sensitive ticket availability, we recommend polling this endpoint periodically rather than relying on cached data.

items.question: Can I get Theater Detail for past shows?
items.answer: Yes. Past shows retain their full detail records including complete lineups, setlist info, and seitansai data. Pass a past show_id to retrieve the complete historical record for any show that JKT48Connect has data for.

items.question: Does the API include special show types like request hour?
items.answer: Yes. Request hour events, unit shows, and joint performances are all included with their respective show_type field. Theater Detail for these special shows includes the same comprehensive data as regular shows, with additional fields specific to the show type where available.
